mappings.rationaleAI: Aristotle's biological investigations: classification, reproduction, heredity (A.1.b).
Pliny's Natural History: Roman-era synthesis of biological knowledge (A.1.b).
2,000 years of anatomy from Galen to Vesalius (A.1.b-A.1.d).
Galen: Greco-Roman anatomy (A.1.b), Islamic and medieval influence (A.1.c).

mappings.episodeTitle: Goethe and the Science of the Enlightenment
mappings.rationaleAI: Goethe's morphology and comparative anatomy: history of biological sciences.
Microscope revealed microorganisms and cells, transforming biological investigation.

mappings.episodeTitle: Darwin: On the Origins of Charles Darwin
mappings.rationaleAI: Darwin's formation as a scientist: history of biological sciences.
Genetics from Mendel through DNA: major chapter in biological sciences.
Linnaeus's binomial nomenclature: systematic taxonomy from Aristotelian origins (A.1.b).
Taxonomy from Linnaeus through cladistics: classification as biological discipline.
Women in Enlightenment science: botanical knowledge and classification.
Wallace: co-discovery of natural selection, founding biogeography.

mappings.episodeTitle: Darwin: The Voyage of the Beagle
mappings.rationaleAI: Darwin's Beagle voyage: defining episode in biological sciences.
Botany and imperial expansion: institutional history of biological sciences.
X-ray crystallography enabling molecular biology including DNA structure.
